thinking_in_questions_and_answers.label: Thinking in Questions and Answers
thinking_in_questions_and_answers.summary: Explore how questions translate into SQL queries using the students_intro dataset.

quiz1.title: Basic SQL Query Structure
quiz1.prompt: What is the purpose of the `SELECT` statement in SQL?
quiz1.hint: Think about what you want to do when you want to see data.

help.concept: The `SELECT` statement is used to retrieve data from one or more columns in a table.
help.hint_1: The `SELECT` statement is used to view data.
help.hint_2: It is the first part of a query that specifies which data you want to see.

options.a: To delete data from a table
options.b: To retrieve data from a table
options.c: To update data in a table
options.d: To insert data into a table

quiz2.title: Columns in a SQL Query
quiz2.prompt: Which columns can you retrieve using the following query: `SELECT name, grade FROM students;`? Choose all that apply.
quiz2.hint: Look at the columns listed after `SELECT`.

help.concept: The columns listed after `SELECT` are the ones that will be retrieved.
help.hint_1: Check the columns specified in the query.
help.hint_2: Only the columns explicitly mentioned will be retrieved.

quiz3.title: Order of SQL Query Clauses
quiz3.prompt: Arrange the SQL query clauses in the correct order.
quiz3.hint: Read each piece and arrange them in the order the statement should be understood.

help.concept: The pieces should form a valid statement in a logical order.
help.hint_1: Start with the piece that introduces the idea or action.
help.hint_2: Place dependent pieces after the part they describe or complete.

quiz4.title: Completing a SQL Query
quiz4.prompt: Complete the SQL query to retrieve the `age` of students: `SELECT [blank1] FROM students;`
quiz4.hint: Think about which column you want to retrieve.

help.concept: The column you want to retrieve should be placed after `SELECT`.
help.hint_1: You are interested in the `the missing term` of students.
help.hint_2: The correct column name is `the missing term`.

quiz5.title: Retrieve Student Names
quiz5.prompt: Write a SQL query to retrieve the names of all students from the `students` table.
quiz5.hint: Use the lesson explanation and the wording of this question to narrow the answer.

help.concept: This support text was repaired because the original wording revealed the answer too directly.
help.hint_1: Check the table name and selected columns in the SQL editor.
help.hint_2: Choose the option that directly matches the question without relying on answer wording.

quiz6.title: SQL Query Result
quiz6.prompt: What will be the result of the query `SELECT city FROM students;`?
quiz6.hint: Look at the column specified after `SELECT`.

help.concept: The query retrieves the data from the column specified after `SELECT`.
help.hint_2: The result will be a list of cities.

quiz7.title: Understanding SQL Syntax
quiz7.prompt: Which of the following are valid SQL statements? Choose all that apply.
quiz7.hint: Focus on statements that retrieve data.

help.concept: Valid SQL statements for this module involve selecting data.
help.hint_2: Statements that modify data are not covered in this module.

quiz8.title: SQL Query Construction
quiz8.prompt: Arrange the parts of a SQL query to form a correct statement.

help.concept: A SQL query starts with `SELECT` followed by `FROM`.

quiz9.title: SQL Query Completion
quiz9.prompt: Complete the SQL query to retrieve the `grade` of students: `SELECT [blank1] FROM students;`
quiz9.hint: Think about which column you want to retrieve.

help.concept: The column you want to retrieve should be placed after `SELECT`.
help.hint_1: You are interested in the `the missing term` of students.
help.hint_2: The correct column name is `the missing term`.

sketch1.title: Introduction to SQL Queries
sketch1.bodyMarkdown:
SQL is a language used to interact with databases. It allows you to ask questions about the data stored in tables. For example, if you want to know the names of all students in a database, you can use a SQL query to retrieve that information. 

Here's a simple SQL query:

```
SELECT name FROM students;
```

This query selects the `name` column from the `students` table. Try it yourself by writing this query in the SQL editor and clicking 'Run query'.

sketch2.title: Understanding the SELECT Statement
sketch2.bodyMarkdown:
The `SELECT` statement is used to specify which columns you want to retrieve from a table. It is the most basic and essential part of any SQL query. 

For example, to get the names and grades of students, you can write:

```
SELECT name, grade FROM students;
```

This query will return a list of names and their corresponding grades. Try modifying the query to include the `age` column as well.
